- Reticulated giraffes have large, well-defined polygonal patches — typically ~180–220 visible spots.
- Masai giraffes have smaller, more jagged, numerous spots — often ~300–400.
- Northern and Southern giraffes fall somewhere in between, ~200–300.

So: 120,000 × 240 ≈ **28.8 million**, which I round to **29,000,000**.

Big caveats: "spot" is poorly defined (do you count tiny secondary spots inside larger ones? spots on the lower legs that fade out?), and counts per individual giraffe in published studies vary widely.
